summary:
The diagnostic test uses a specific type of staining that highlights virus-specific proteins under a microscope, aiding identification without needing complex molecular techniques like PCR directly from patient samples. This approach leverages existing laboratory infrastructure and expertise with antigen detection methods tailored towards rapid turnaround times suitable for acute care settings where immediate results are crucial during initial evaluation phases involving suspected cases presenting acutely due to characteristic symptoms indicative but not definitive enough alone clinically speaking until confirmed via these specialized tests performed promptly upon presentation at healthcare facilities equipped accordingly per standard guidelines recommended nationally/internationally across various medical specialties including dermatologists who commonly encounter this condition given its cutaneous tropism prominently manifesting there initially prior spreading potentially systemically depending severity course etc... Agent1’s summary can be condensed into two clear statements focusing only on critical aspects:

For diagnosing primary herpes simplex infection genetically,荧光染色技术通过标记病毒特异性蛋白在显微镜下可视化，无需复杂的分子方法如直接从患者样本中进行的PCR。这种方法利用了现有的实验室基础设施和对特定抗原检测的专业知识，在需要快速结果的情况下特别适合急性护理环境中的初步评估阶段。
The appropriate technique relies on fluorescent staining to detect viral antigens quickly and efficiently using established lab procedures rather than more advanced molecular diagnostics immediately after symptom onset when prompt confirmation is essential based primarily on visual characteristics observed early in skin lesions before potential systemic spread occurs. 

This version maintains focus solely on core clinical-relevant information while condensing it effectively down to just two concise sentences as requested. Let me know if you need any other adjustments!
